description_long: This is a round, wide-mouthed ceramic bowl with a slightly flared rim and a bulbous body. The surface is matte and shows a range of earthy tones, including brown, tan, and some darker patches, likely from age or firing marks. There are visible spots and discolorations, suggesting it has been used or exposed to the elements over time. Two small rectangular labels are affixed to one side, possibly for cataloging or identification purposes. The bowl appears handmade, with subtle irregularities in its shape and finish, and no visible handles or decorative motifs. The interior is dark, and the bowl is empty.
description: A round, wide-mouthed ceramic bowl with a matte, earth-toned surface and visible age spots. It has a simple, utilitarian design with no decorative patterns.
description_view_0: This view shows the bowl's side with two small white labels and prominent dark spotting on the surface.
description_view_1: This angle highlights a smoother, less spotted area with a greenish discoloration and no visible labels.
description_view_2: The bowl appears more uniformly colored from this side, with minimal spotting and no labels visible.
description_view_3: This view again shows the side with the two white labels, but with less pronounced spotting than view 0.

license_info.creator_display_name: The Royal Museums of Art and History
